sourceMovie.title: The Contractor
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Contractor (2022) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Tarik Saleh's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Wick
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Contractor clicked for you, John Wick (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Sicario
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Contractor clicked for you, Sicario (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Contractor?
faq.answer: Try John Wick, Sicario, The Transporter—each shares DNA with The Contractor while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Contractor?
faq.answer: The Contractor (2022) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Contractor worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
